hero.title: Audubon
hero.reason: In honor of Audubon Day (April 26), celebrating naturalist John James Audubon's legacy, this biographical drama chronicles the life of the pioneering ornithologist and artist who revolutionized our understanding of birds and nature through his meticulous illustrations and observations.
hero.blurb: A visually stunning tribute to the man who taught America to see birds as subjects worthy of scientific study and artistic beauty. Audubon's passion for nature and relentless pursuit of knowledge across the frontier remains inspiring and deeply moving.

released.title: A Serious Man
released.reason: Released today in 2009, this Coen Brothers masterpiece about an ordinary man's unraveling life during suburban Jewish Minnesota crisis remains a darkly comic meditation on faith, uncertainty, and the absurdity of everyday suffering.
released.blurb: A brilliantly twisted examination of job loss, family chaos, and spiritual desperation wrapped in pitch-black comedy. Michael Stuhlbarg delivers a career-defining performance as a man desperately seeking meaning in a meaningless universe.

birthday.title: In Good Company
birthday.reason: Happy birthday to Chloë Sevigny (born April 26, 1974). This romantic comedy perfectly showcases her wit and charm as a young woman navigating love and career ambitions in the high-pressure world of magazine publishing.
birthday.blurb: A smart, breezy romantic comedy elevated by great chemistry and witty banter about age gaps and workplace dynamics. Chloë Sevigny brings intelligence and warmth to a character caught between two very different men.

awful.title: Birdemic: Shock and Terror
awful.reason: A legendarily incompetent creature feature where rubber birds attack California with all the terror of angry pool toys, creating an unintentional masterpiece of how not to make a movie.
awful.blurb: Prepare yourself for some of the worst dialogue, cheapest special effects, and most baffling creative choices ever committed to film. It's so spectacularly terrible that it has achieved cult immortality as peak bad-movie entertainment.
